text.title: Chroma Basic Usage
text.summary: Collection creation, upsert, query, update, and delete patterns with rules for IDs and distance-to-similarity conversion.

rule.statement: Set the distance metric at collection creation time; it cannot be changed without recreating the collection.
rule.rationale: Chroma bakes the metric into the HNSW index. Changing it later requires a full data migration.

rule.statement: Always use string IDs; integer IDs must be stringified before passing to Chroma.
rule.rationale: Chroma enforces string-typed IDs; passing integers causes a type error.

example.code:
import chromadb
# In-memory (lost on process exit — dev/test only) client = chromadb.Client()
# Persistent (SQLite-backed — single-process only) client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")
collection = client.get_or_create_collection( name="documents", metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"}, # cosine | l2 | ip )
example.note: Use get_or_create_collection so re-runs are idempotent.

rule.statement: Convert Chroma distances to similarity scores before returning to callers: similarity = 1 - distance for cosine space.
rule.rationale: Chroma returns distances (lower = more similar) not similarity scores (higher = more similar). Callers expect similarity semantics.

example.code:
# Upsert (add or update) collection.add( ids=["doc1", "doc2"], embeddings=[emb1, emb2], metadatas=[{"source": "a.pdf"}, {"source": "b.pdf"}], documents=["Text 1", "Text 2"], )
# Query results = collection.query( query_embeddings=[query_embedding], n_results=10, where={"source": {"$eq": "a.pdf"}}, where_document={"$contains": "keyword"}, include=["documents", "metadatas", "distances"], )
# Convert distances to similarity similarities = [1 - d for d in results["distances"][0]]
# Update existing documents collection.update(ids=["doc1"], embeddings=[new_emb], metadatas=[{"source": "updated.pdf"}])
# Delete collection.delete(ids=["doc1"]) collection.delete(where={"source": "old.pdf"})

antipattern.description: Using in-memory chromadb.Client() in a pipeline that restarts between ingestion and retrieval.
antipattern.reason: In-memory client loses all data on process exit. Use PersistentClient with a directory path.

antipattern.description: Running concurrent writes from multiple processes against the same PersistentClient directory.
antipattern.reason: SQLite allows only one writer at a time; concurrent writes from separate processes corrupt the database.

antipattern.description: Storing full document text as metadata values (kilobytes per document).
antipattern.reason: Chroma loads all metadata into memory; large payloads bloat RAM and slow queries. Use the documents field or store large content externally.

section.title: Template Reference
section.reference: ChromaStore wrapper with upsert, search, delete and similarity conversion
